name: A Pontenova Mines Trail
name_original: Hornos y minas de Vilaoudriz – circular desde A Pontenova
description: Jog a moderate 4.8-mile route through A Pontenova, exploring historic Vilaoudriz Kilns and Consuelo Mine with 868 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Río Eo offer diverse landscapes, transitioning from the Cantabrian Sea coastline to rolling mountains and valleys. The region is characterized by rich riparian woodlands, deciduous forests, and the significant Eo Estuary, a haven for wildlife. Trails often follow riverbanks, traverse forests, and provide views of the estuary and surrounding green meadows. This varied terrain, from coastal paths to more challenging routes in the hills, caters to different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Río Eo The most popular jogging route is Toques Waterfalls Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This route features a notable waterfall and offers a challenging elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is A Pontenova Mines Trail, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This running route explores the area's mining heritage and offers varied scenery. Local runners also love the Isla Pancha Lighthouse loop from Castropol, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas with views of the lighthouse, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Río Eo is defined by coastal paths, estuary views, and forest trails, with options for both easy riverside runs and more challenging routes in the rolling hills. The network of running routes provides diverse terrain for all ability levels. The routes in Río Eo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 8 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Río Eo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Río Eo region?
A: The Río Eo region offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 60 different trails available for joggers. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, the Río Eo region has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed jog. You'll find paths along the estuary and converted old railway lines, offering relatively flat and scenic options. There are 5 easy routes available, providing gentle terrain to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in the Río Eo area?
A: Absolutely! For those who enjoy the sight and sound of waterfalls, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991266661/toques-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toques Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This 3.4-mile (5.5 km) route features a notable waterfall and offers a rewarding experience amidst nature.

Q: Are there running trails that explore the region's mining heritage?
A: Yes, you can explore the area's rich mining history while jogging. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991656560/a-pontenova-mines-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Pontenova Mines Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) path that takes you through varied scenery and past remnants of the region's mining past, including the old mining railway line in San Tirso de Abres.

Q: What kind of coastal running experiences can I expect around Río Eo?
A: The Río Eo region offers spectacular coastal running experiences, particularly around the estuary where the river meets the Cantabrian Sea. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24610216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Pancha Lighthouse loop from Castropol&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning views of the Ría del Eo and its unique marine environment. You can also find paths near iconic spots like Penarronda Beach.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Río Eo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Río Eo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24610216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Pancha Lighthouse loop from Castropol&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.3-mile (10.1 km) circular route offering coastal views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I might see along the running trails?
A: As you jog through Río Eo, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. Near some routes, you might spot the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;, the industrial heritage site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz&lt;/a&gt;, or the cultural insights offered by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3529143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cargadoiro Ethnographic Park&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/607696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Chapel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3785204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Damián Fort&lt;/a&gt; also add historical charm to the landscape.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Río Eo?
A: The Río Eo region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 8 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil estuary paths to challenging forest trails, and the stunning natural beauty of the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The variety of terrain and well-maintained routes are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes in Río Eo. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fishing wall – Old Rinlo Shellfish Hatchery loop from Castropol&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 13-mile (21 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; covers over 12.5 miles (20 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while running in Río Eo?
A: The Río Eo region, part of a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offers a rich tapestry of natural features. You'll encounter diverse ecosystems, from the Cantabrian Sea coastline with its cliffs and marsh vegetation to rich riparian woodlands of oak, holly, and willow. The tranquil Eo Estuary is a haven for birdwatching, and further inland, you'll find rolling mountains and extensive forests of chestnut and birch.

Q: Are there running routes that offer views of the famous Isla Pancha Lighthouse?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent views of the iconic Isla Pancha Lighthouse. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Pancha Lighthouse – Pancha Island Lighthouse loop from Castropol&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.8-mile (6.1 km) route that specifically highlights this landmark, offering picturesque coastal scenery.

Q: Can I combine my run with opportunities to see wildlife?
A: Absolutely. The Río Eo Estuary is a designated Wetland of International Importance and a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). Jogging along the estuary paths, you'll have excellent opportunities for birdwatching, especially for migratory and wintering waterfowl, making it a great option for wildlife enthusiasts.
tips:
The Vilaoudriz kilns, made up of five kilns built in different stages, are the largest set of calcination kilns in the northwest of the peninsula and a visit is highly recommended.

The two oldest were built in brick, while the other three used masonry.

With 11 meters high and 4 meters in diameter each, and four loading entrances, this construction also preserves a mine entrance from where the mineral extracted from the Vieiro Mine came.

The Consuleo Mines are a unique space, integrated into nature, which values the mining past of the town of A Pontenova, in the province of Lugo. Without a doubt, a spectacular place to enter and enjoy the second largest cavity in Galicia.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ovens and mines of Vilaoudriz trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from A Pontenova, you can typically find parking within the town to begin your journey. Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but general parking in A Pontenova would be your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Ovens and mines of Vilaoudriz trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the route with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with historical structures or other visitors, and to be mindful of local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. During these seasons, the weather is typically more stable, making for a comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Ovens and mines of Vilaoudriz circular trail itself. You are free to explore the route at your leisure.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, including unpaved sections, paved areas, hiking paths, roads, and streets. You'll encounter approximately 1.45 km of hiking path, 533 meters of road, and 467 meters of street, along with smaller segments of asphalt. Good sure-footedness is recommended due to these mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in industrial heritage. You'll encounter the impressive Hornos de Vilaoudriz (Vilaoudriz Kilns), which are the largest collection of calcination kilns in the northwest Iberian Peninsula. You can also explore the Consuelo Mine, the second-largest cavity in Galicia. The trail also showcases other elements of 20th-century iron mining, such as old railway facilities, warehouses, and mining tunnels.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 7.7 km long and typically takes around 1 hour and 3 minutes to jog, depending on your pace and how often you stop to explore the historical sites.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate and requires a good level of fitness for jogging. While not overly strenuous, its varied terrain and elevation gain of 264 meters mean it's more challenging than an easy route, so beginners should be prepared for a moderate workout.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with the Vía Verde do Eo for about 5% of its length. It is also sometimes referred to as "Ruta dos Fornos" (Route of the Kilns) and is part of a longer "Ruta das Minas" (Route of the Mines), indicating connections to a broader network of mining heritage trails.

faqs.question: Are there any guided tours or special activities available for the mining sites?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are guided hiking routes that specifically include entry to the Consuelo Mine and may even offer an optional zip-line descent. These guided tours typically cover a distance of about 4 km and provide a deeper insight into the mining history.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, it's advisable to wear appropriate jogging shoes with good grip. Layers of clothing are recommended to adapt to changing weather conditions. Don't forget water, especially on warmer days, and a camera to capture the unique industrial heritage and natural scenery.
